\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:MSTOOptpar.AggressiveRefinement}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MSTOOptpar.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{AggressiveRefinement}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{mol}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{fname}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{trials}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{10}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{tol}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{1e\sphinxhyphen{}16}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{fci}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{True}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{itermax}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{100}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Performs a local, gradient\sphinxhyphen{}free iterative search (hill\sphinxhyphen{}climbing) to fine\sphinxhyphen{}tune 
basis set parameters. This exploitation phase provides the precision 
necessary for scientific accuracy.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{mol}} \textendash{} Best molecule object from the global search phase.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{fname}} \textendash{} Path to save the refined candidate.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{trials}} \textendash{} Number of independent refinement trials.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{tol}} \textendash{} Energy tolerance for local convergence.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{fci}} \textendash{} Whether to include FCI results in the saved output.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{itermax}} \textendash{} Max iterations permitted per refinement trial.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Example}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{mol\PYGZus{}best} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{loadBSI}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MOBS\PYGZus{}Li\PYGZus{}msto\PYGZhy{}03g.pickle}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{mol\PYGZus{}CISD}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{mol}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{AggressiveRefinement}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{mol\PYGZus{}best}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MOBS\PYGZus{}Li\PYGZus{}refined.pickle}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{trials}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{DumpStandardBasis() (in module MSTOOptpar)@\spxentry{DumpStandardBasis()}\spxextra{in module MSTOOptpar}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:MSTOOptpar.DumpStandardBasis}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MSTOOptpar.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{DumpStandardBasis}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{element}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{basis}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dirname}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{\textquotesingle{}./\textquotesingle{}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{forcedump}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Create the molecule object for a molecule in a given basis and dump in a file. 
A new file will be created only if it does not exist. 
The format of the filename will be \sphinxtitleref{BS\_\textless{}element\textgreater{}\_\textless{}basis\textgreater{}.pickle} and stored in the specified directory.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{element}} (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{str}}) \textendash{} A molecule defined in \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MSTOOpt.moldict()}}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{basis}} (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{str}}) \textendash{} A basis name defined in \sphinxtitleref{basis\_set\_exchange package} for which the energy is to be computed.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dirname}} (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{str}}) \textendash{} The directory where the molecule object is to be dumped.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Example}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{DumpStandardBasis}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{H}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{sto\PYGZhy{}4g}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{dirname}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{./}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{FetchBestMol() (in module MSTOOptpar)@\spxentry{FetchBestMol()}\spxextra{in module MSTOOptpar}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:MSTOOptpar.FetchBestMol}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MSTOOptpar.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{FetchBestMol}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{element}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{k}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dirname}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{\textquotesingle{}./\textquotesingle{}}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Fetches the best available basis for optimization of \sphinxtitleref{element} for cardinal number \sphinxtitleref{k}.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The order is as follows for a cardinal number \sphinxtitleref{k}:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
MSTO\sphinxhyphen{}kG

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
STO\sphinxhyphen{}kG

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
MSTO\sphinxhyphen{}(k\sphinxhyphen{}1)G

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
STO\sphinxhyphen{}(k\sphinxhyphen{}1)G

\item {} 
\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
It is best to ensure that at least the STO\sphinxhyphen{}2G basis is available.

\sphinxAtStartPar
To do:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
if k\textless{}2 fetch the sto\sphinxhyphen{}2g basis within this function.

\end{itemize}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{LogicalRefinement() (in module MSTOOptpar)@\spxentry{LogicalRefinement()}\spxextra{in module MSTOOptpar}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:MSTOOptpar.LogicalRefinement}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MSTOOptpar.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{LogicalRefinement}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{element}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Nmax}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{trials}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{20}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{tol}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{1e\sphinxhyphen{}16}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{trials\_mu}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{15}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{trials\_co}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{20}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{prefix}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{\textquotesingle{}MOBS\textquotesingle{}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{dirname}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{\textquotesingle{}./\textquotesingle{}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{fci}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{True}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{itermax}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{25}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{agg\_trial}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{True}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{ga\_trial}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{True}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{kmax}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{12}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Refine the MSTO basis sets generated. For 3 \textless{}= k \textless{}= 11 if the FCI energy for a given k is larger than that for k\sphinxhyphen{}1, an optimization is performed.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{mol}} (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pyscf.gto.mole.Mole}}) \textendash{} the \sphinxtitleref{pyscf} molecule object

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Nmax}} (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{int}}) \textendash{} Population of candidates to consider in the optimization

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{trials}} (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{str}}) \textendash{} The number of generations to run

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{tol}} (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{float}}) \textendash{} Tolerance of energy for convergence

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{tol\_mu}} \textendash{} Number of mutations to consider under each trial

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{trials\_co}} (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{int}}) \textendash{} Number of crossovers to consider after each mutation

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{prefix}} (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{str}} (optional. Defaults to \sphinxtitleref{MOBS})) \textendash{} Prefix of the pickled database

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{dirname}} (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{str}} (optional. Defaults to \sphinxtitleref{./})) \textendash{} Directory to look for the dumped database files.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Nothing

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar

\sphinxlineitem{Example}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:MSTOOptpar.OptimizeElement}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MSTOOptpar.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{OptimizeElement}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{mol}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{Nmax}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{fname}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{trials}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{10}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{tol}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{1e\sphinxhyphen{}10}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{trials\_mu}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{100}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{trials\_co}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{100}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Optimizes basis set coefficients using a hybrid Memetic Algorithm.
Evolves a population of basis sets toward a lower RCISD ground state energy.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{mol}} (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pyscf.gto.mole.Mole}}) \textendash{} Initial PySCF molecule object used as a template.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{Nmax}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}) \textendash{} Population size of candidates for global exploration.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{fname}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{str}}) \textendash{} Path to the pickle file where the best candidate is updated.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{trials}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}) \textendash{} Number of major optimization cycles.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{tol}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{float}}) \textendash{} Energy tolerance for population convergence (Hartree).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{trials\_mu}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}) \textendash{} Number of mutation attempts per major cycle.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{trials\_co}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}) \textendash{} Number of crossover operations per mutation trial.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Tuple of (final population, generation history, energy history).

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
tuple

\sphinxlineitem{Example}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{element} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{Li}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{mol} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{moldict}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{sto\PYGZhy{}3g}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{n}{element}\PYG{p}{]}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{fname} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MOBS\PYGZus{}Li\PYGZus{}msto\PYGZhy{}03g.pickle}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{pop}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{gen}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{en} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{OptimizeElement}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{mol}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{fname}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{trials}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{l+m+mi}{2}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
An instance of the computed basis sets can be dumped as a pickle file. It will be useful if one wishes to reuse these values for further improvement.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{BSI\_instance}} (Prefereably {\hyperref[\detokenize{api:MSTOOptpar.getEnergies}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{getEnergies}}}}}. (\sphinxstyleemphasis{Note:} It will not raise an error otherwise.)) \textendash{} The python object that will be pickled for easy retrieval later. This is used internally as the {\hyperref[\detokenize{api:MSTOOptpar.getEnergies}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{getEnergies}}}}} object for the optimized basis set. The “BSI” stands for basis set improved.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{fname}} (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{str}}) \textendash{} The filename where the pickled object will be dumped.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Nothing

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
None

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{getEnergies() (in module MSTOOptpar)@\spxentry{getEnergies()}\spxextra{in module MSTOOptpar}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:MSTOOptpar.getEnergies}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MSTOOptpar.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{getEnergies}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{mol}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{nroots}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{4}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{fci}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{False}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{cisd}\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{default_value}{True}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Retrieve the Hartree\sphinxhyphen{}Fock, FCI, and CISD energies for a molecule.
The method ensures the molecule object is built locally to maintain 
consistency in parallel environments.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{mol}} (\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{pyscf.gto.mole.Mole}}) \textendash{} The molecule whose energies are to be determined.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{nroots}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{int}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{defaults to 4.}}) \textendash{} Number of roots for the FCI solver.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{fci}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{defaults to False.}}) \textendash{} Whether to compute Full Configuration Interaction energy.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{cisd}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{bool}}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{, }}\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{defaults to True.}}) \textendash{} Whether to compute Configuration Interaction Singles and Doubles energy.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Dictionary containing ‘HF’, ‘FCI’, and ‘CISD’ energies.

\sphinxlineitem{Return type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
dict

\sphinxlineitem{Example}
\end{description}\end{quote}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{mol} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{moldict}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{sto\PYGZhy{}3g}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{H2}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{]}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{energies} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{getEnergies}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{mol}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{cisd}\PYG{o}{=}\PYG{k+kc}{True}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{energies}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{CISD}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{\PYGZhy{}1.11671432537233}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{putcoeffs() (in module MSTOOptpar)@\spxentry{putcoeffs()}\spxextra{in module MSTOOptpar}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{api:MSTOOptpar.putcoeffs}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MSTOOptpar.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{putcoeffs}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{iden}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{b}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Creates a new basis set for a \sphinxtitleref{pyscf.gto.mole.Mole} object from the element/shell identifiers and the array of basis coefficients. These need not be created by hand (you can if you want to and know what you are doing). This function was created to modify the basis of the molecule object and then inject the modified basis back into the molecule. The best way to use it would be to extract the identifier and basis set array using \sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{MSTOkG.MSTOOpt.getcoeffs()}}, modify the basis appropriately and then use this function to generate the new basis. This new basis should be used to replace the basis in the \sphinxtitleref{pyscf.gto.mole.Mole} object (and subsequently build it).
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{iden}} (\sphinxstyleliteralemphasis{\sphinxupquote{list}}) \textendash{} A list of element name and shell identifiers

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
